title: Detect Log Bursts
description: Given a log file with one Unix timestamp per line, detect time windows where the log event count exceeds a threshold. Group events into 1-minute windows (window_start = timestamp // 60 * 60) and return windows where the count exceeds the threshold as (window_start, count) tuples.
constraints: Each line contains a single Unix timestamp integer
1-minute windows are defined as: window_start = timestamp // 60 * 60
Only return windows where count is strictly greater than threshold
Threshold defaults to 3 events per window
Return results sorted by window_start ascending

examples.explanation: The first minute (1700000000-1700000059) contains 5 events which exceeds threshold=3. The second minute contains only 2 events and is not returned.

starter_code.python:
def detect_bursts(log_content: str, threshold: int = 3) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
    pass

test_cases.description: First window has 5 events exceeding threshold=3
Each window has exactly 1 event, none exceed threshold
Two windows each with 5 events both exceed threshold
